summary: c=2 full-attention fallback sweep after auto selected-c1 MoE: per-row full-attention fallback is generated-token green and slightly faster than native batch full attention when combined with selected-c1 MoE, but it is explicitly not native c-aware/retained and still below c1. Native batch full-attention remains the retained path.

variants.name: per_row_full_attention
batch_execution_blockers: native c>N decode currently supports compact physical-slot-ordered rows; full-attention batch context is native only for BF16 KV and context < 1024
full-attention decode used a per-row fallback, so this is not native c-aware decode

retained_claim_blockers: per-row full-attention fallback is not native c-aware decode and has explicit batch_execution blockers
per-row full-attention best run remains below c1 aggregate throughput
native batch full-attention remains required for retained c>N rows even though the per-row diagnostic is faster at c=2

conclusion.reason: Per-row full-attention plus selected-c1 MoE is a useful diagnostic and reaches 0.967x c1, but it is non-retained/non-c-aware. No graph replay option is currently wired in retained-bench; this iteration records focused full-attention fallback evidence instead of changing code.

commands.sweep: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py ... with variants default, --batch-decode-full-attn-path per_row, per_row plus --batch-decode-linear-output-path selected_c1, and per_row plus --batch-decode-linear-output-path batch
commands.profile_per_row: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace --output-format csv -d /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-fullattn-perrow-selected-moe-profile-iter116 -- env HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py ... --batch-decode-full-attn-path per_row --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-fullattn-perrow-selected-moe-profiled-iter116.json
